mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS year-over-year growth does not show a statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window (p>0.10), this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, the labor market tightens beyond full employment, leading to wage inflation pressure that compresses corporate margins and signals an impending cyclical downturn, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return after UNRATE < 4.0% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p<0.05) relative to other periods, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2% and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below the inflation rate, monetary policy is accommodative, fueling asset price inflation and positive equity returns. The joint condition produces a stronger signal than either series alone.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward SPX return in periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY does not exceed the unconditional mean by more than 2% (absolute), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its 2017 baseline signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, leading to negative earnings revisions and downward pressure on equities. This signal is particularly powerful during the early stages of an industrial recession when the economy is still above potential output.
failure_condition: If the predictive accuracy of INDPRO below 100 for negative 60-day returns is not significantly better than random (balanced accuracy < 0.55) over out-of-sample periods, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and earnings, leading to positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon. The joint behavior of these two series amplifies the signal.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from PAYEMS and UNRATE does not yield an average 5-day forward return > 0.2% with a Sharpe ratio > 0.5 over a 5-year backtest, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% represents a tightening monetary stance that raises the discount rate on equity cash flows, compresses valuations, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns after FEDFUNDS crosses 5% are not negative more than 60% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth above 2% year-over-year signals strong economic expansion, driving corporate profits and equity prices higher over the next quarter. GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity and its trend is a reliable predictor of equity returns.
failure_condition: If the correlation between GDPC1 YoY growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p>0.05) over a 10-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 1500 thousand units indicate an overheating housing market that often precedes a correction in residential investment and consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns over the next month as the cycle turns.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns after HOUST exceeds 1500k are not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p<0.10), reject.
